15% of Americans say they’d be willing to work for an AI boss, according to new poll
articles.description: According to a Quinnipiac University poll, 15% of Americans say they'd be willing to have a job where their direct supervisor was an AI program that assigned tasks and set schedules.

articles.title: Popular AI gateway startup LiteLLM ditches controversial startup Delve
articles.description: LiteLLM had obtained two security compliance certifications via Delve and fell victim to some horrific credential-stealing malware last week.

articles.title: Former Coatue partner raises huge $65M seed for enterprise AI agent startup
articles.description: A few things turned investors' heads and drew them to participate in such a big round out of the gate.

articles.title: As more Americans adopt AI tools, fewer say they can trust the results
articles.description: AI adoption is rising in the U.S., but trust remains low, with most Americans concerned about transparency, regulation, and the technology’s broader societal impact, according to a new Quinnipiac poll.

articles.title: What we’re looking for in Startup Battlefield 2026 and how to put your best application forward
articles.description: Here's what we're looking for in your Startup Battlefield 200 application and how to make sure your application reflects it.

articles.title: Delve whistleblower strikes again, with alleged receipts about ‘fake compliance’
articles.description: Shortly after Delve's founder published a lengthy denial of allegations, while promising changes, the accuser came forward with more alleged details.

articles.title: Meta starts testing a premium subscription on Instagram
articles.description: Among the premium features on Instagram is the ability to view a Story without the poster seeing that you viewed it and the option to see how many people have rewatched your Story.

articles.title: Bluesky’s new AI tool Attie is already the most blocked account other than J. D. Vance
articles.description: More than 125,000 Bluesky users have blocked Attie in just a few days,  sending a strong message from the user base.

articles.title: Apple will hide your email address from apps and websites, but not cops
articles.description: Demands for Apple customer records by federal agents in recent months underscore the privacy limitations of email.

articles.title: Uber is buying Berlin startup Blacklane to bolster its ‘Elite’ offering
articles.description: It's a notable exit for Blacklane, which has raised more than $100 million from backers like Mercedes-Benz and Sixt.

articles.title: Mantis Biotech is making ‘digital twins’ of humans to help solve medicine’s data availability problem
articles.description: Mantis takes disparate sources of data to make synthetic datasets that can be used to build so-called "digital twins" of the human body, representing anatomy, physiology and behavior.

articles.title: ScaleOps raises $130M to improve computing efficiency amid AI demand
articles.description: ScaleOps just raised $130M to tackle GPU shortages and soaring AI cloud costs by automating infrastructure in real time.

articles.title: AI chip startup Rebellions raises $400 million at $2.3B valuation in pre-IPO round
articles.description: The startup, which is planning to go public later this year, designs chips specifically for AI inference, another challenger to Nvidia's dominance.

articles.title: Mistral AI raises $830M in debt to set up a data center near Paris
articles.description: Mistral aims to start operating the data center by the second quarter of 2026.

articles.title: Qodo raises $70M for code verification as AI coding scales
articles.description: As AI floods software development with code, Qodo is betting the real challenge is making sure it actually works.

articles.title: Starcloud raises $170 million Series A to build data centers in space
articles.description: Starcloud becomes the fastest Y Combinator startup to reach unicorn status, just 17 months after demo day.

articles.title: Why OpenAI really shut down Sora
articles.description: OpenAI's decision last week to shut down Sora, its AI video-generation tool, just six months after releasing it to the public, raised immediate suspicions. The app had invited users to upload their own faces — so was this some kind of elaborate data grab?

digest:
## 🔬 Major Technical Developments

### Rust's next-generation trait solver
URL: https://lwn.net/SubscriberLink/1063124/81483612b1c8a493/
Rust compiler infrastructure getting a foundational upgrade that will unlock more expressive type-level programming.
The new trait solver addresses long-standing limitations in how Rust reasons about generic code, which matters because it removes friction for library authors building zero-cost abstractions. This is infrastructure work that compounds — expect more sophisticated async runtimes and embedded frameworks to follow.

### Vulnerability Research Is Cooked
URL: https://sockpuppet.org/blog/2026/03/30/vulnerability-research-is-cooked/
Security research economics are breaking down as bug bounties stagnate while exploit complexity increases.
The piece surfaces a structural problem: finding vulnerabilities now requires more specialized tooling and time, but payouts haven't kept pace. This creates perverse incentives — researchers either exit to offense-focused shops or stop disclosing altogether. Enterprise security teams should expect less coverage from independent researchers and budget accordingly.

### Android Developer Verification
URL: https://android-developers.googleblog.com/2026/03/android-developer-verification-rolling-out-to-all-developers.html
Google mandating identity verification for all Play Store developers — a move to reduce fraud and malware.
This raises the barrier to entry for bad actors but also adds friction for indie developers and those in regions with documentation challenges. Expect short-term reduction in low-quality app submissions; watch for whether this pushes legitimate developers toward sideloading or alternative stores.

## 💼 Business & Market Signals

### AI chip startup Rebellions raises $400 million at $2.3B valuation in pre-IPO round
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/30/ai-chip-startup-rebellions-raises-400-million-at-2-3b-valuation-in-pre-ipo-round/
Korean inference chip maker positioning for public markets as Nvidia alternatives gain traction.
Rebellions is targeting inference specifically — the high-volume, lower-margin workload that hyperscalers want to cost-optimize. A $2.3B pre-IPO valuation signals investor conviction that the inference market will fragment away from Nvidia faster than training. Watch their customer concentration and whether they can land a major cloud provider before listing.

### Mistral AI raises $830M in debt to set up a data center near Paris
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/30/mistral-ai-raises-830m-in-debt-to-set-up-a-data-center-near-paris/
Europe's leading foundation model company taking on debt to own infrastructure — a bet on vertical integration.
Debt financing for a data center is notably different from equity for R&D; Mistral is treating compute as a capital asset rather than a variable cost. This mirrors what we've seen from xAI and suggests the inference economics for frontier models favor ownership over renting. The Q2 2026 timeline is aggressive.

### Starcloud raises $170 million Series A to build data centers in space
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/30/starcloud-raises-170-million-series-ato-build-data-centers-in-space/
YC's fastest unicorn is betting that orbital compute solves cooling and power constraints for AI workloads.
17 months from demo day to unicorn is a velocity signal worth noting regardless of thesis validity. The physics case is real — cooling is free in space — but the economics depend on launch costs continuing to drop. This is either prescient or peak bubble; the differentiation is execution on latency and uptime SLAs.

### ScaleOps raises $130M to improve computing efficiency amid AI demand
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/30/scaleops-130m-series-c-kubernetes-efficiency-ai-demand-funding/
Infrastructure optimization startup scaling up as GPU costs force enterprises to squeeze more from existing capacity.
The thesis here is straightforward: GPU scarcity and cloud AI costs create demand for anything that improves utilization. ScaleOps automates Kubernetes resource allocation in real-time, which is exactly the unsexy plumbing work that prints money during infrastructure crunches. Series C at this size suggests meaningful revenue traction.

## 📋 Case Studies & Deep Dives

### A Couple Million Lines of Haskell: Production Engineering at Mercury
URL: https://blog.haskell.org/a-couple-million-lines-of-haskell/
Mercury's engineering team details how they've scaled a functional programming codebase to production banking infrastructure.
This is rare documentation of Haskell at genuine enterprise scale in a regulated industry. The piece likely covers hiring challenges, runtime performance, and where the type system paid dividends versus created friction. Useful reference for any PE portfolio company evaluating unconventional technology stacks.

### Delve whistleblower strikes again, with alleged receipts about 'fake compliance'
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/30/delve-whistleblower-strikes-again-with-alleged-receipts-about-fake-compliance/
Compliance automation startup accused of fraudulent certifications — LiteLLM already cutting ties.
This is a counterparty risk case study in real time. Delve provided SOC 2 and similar certifications to AI startups who needed them to close enterprise deals. If the fraud allegations hold, customers face immediate compliance gaps and potential contract violations. Watch for downstream audit exposure across Delve's customer base.

### Why OpenAI really shut down Sora
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/29/why-openai-really-shut-down-sora/
OpenAI kills its video generation tool after six months — data practices and liability concerns likely factors.
Sora allowed face uploads, which creates obvious regulatory exposure under GDPR, BIPA, and emerging state privacy laws. The shutdown suggests OpenAI's legal team concluded the liability exceeded the product value. Broader signal: consumer-facing generative AI with biometric inputs is a minefield that even well-resourced players are retreating from.

## ⚡ Signal vs. Noise

The week's capital flows tell a clear story: infrastructure is eating AI. Mistral taking debt for a data center, Rebellions raising pre-IPO for inference chips, Starcloud betting on orbital compute — these are all bets that the bottleneck has shifted from model capability to model deployment economics. Meanwhile, the Delve implosion and OpenAI's Sora shutdown suggest that the compliance and liability frameworks for AI products are tightening faster than companies anticipated. The next 18 months favor picks-and-shovels plays with defensible unit economics over consumer-facing moonshots with unclear regulatory posture.
